name: Etxauri and Guirguillano Loop from Pamplona
name_original: Orkolen - Etxauri gaina – vuelta por el río Arga
description: A challenging 39.1-mile road cycling route to the Summit of Etxauri, returning via the picturesque Arga River, with 3236 feet of gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Orcoyen, located near Pamplona in Navarre, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region features green hills, rivers, and dense forests, with routes ranging from gentle slopes to demanding mountain passes. Its proximity to the Pyrenees provides varied terrain and elevation profiles suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Orcoyen The most popular road cycling route is Etxauri and Guirguillano Loop from Pamplona, a 39.1 miles (62.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route includes significant elevation gain, reaching nearly 990 meters. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Arteta and Alto de Ulzurrun Loop from Pamplona, a moderate 37.7 miles (60.6 km) path. This route offers scenic Pyrenean views. Local road cyclists also love the Pamplona Basin River Loop, a 15.6 miles (25.1 km) trail leading through the Arga River and historic Citadel, often completed in about 1 hour 9 minutes. Road cycling around Orcoyen is defined by green hills, rivers, and mountain passes. The network offers options from gentle slopes to demanding mountain passes, catering to various abilities. The routes in Orcoyen are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 20,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Orcoyen's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Orcoyen?
A: The region around Orcoyen offers a diverse range of landscapes, from "amazingly green" hills that provide challenging climbs to routes that follow rivers and wind through dense forests. Its proximity to the Pyrenees ensures varied terrain and elevation profiles, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Orcoyen?
A: Yes, Orcoyen offers routes for all abilities. For an easier ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986759580/pamplona-basin-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Basin River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option. It's a 15.6-mile (25.1 km) trail with minimal elevation gain (around 190 meters), offering scenic views of the Arga River and the historic Citadel, making it suitable for a more relaxed pace.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs near Orcoyen?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the region provides demanding mountain passes and significant ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984654552/etxauri-and-guirguillano-loop-from-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxauri and Guirguillano Loop from Pamplona&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 39.1-mile (62.9 km) route with nearly 990 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31972862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Erro Ascent – Rabia Bridge, Zubiri loop from Pamplona/Iruña&lt;/a&gt;, which spans almost 99km with 1,190m of elevation gain.

Q: What are the best times of year for road cycling in Orcoyen?
A: The region is generally considered ideal for road cycling, with conditions varying seasonally. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While specific seasonal conditions for winter are not detailed, the diverse terrain suggests that milder periods outside of peak summer heat or potential winter snow would be most enjoyable.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking vistas, especially those ascending to higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986649027/puerto-de-arteta-and-alto-de-ulzurrun-loop-from-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arteta and Alto de Ulzurrun Loop from Pamplona&lt;/a&gt; is known for its scenic Pyrenean views. Additionally, the Metkiritz pass is highlighted for its spectacular natural landscapes.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see while cycling near Orcoyen?
A: You can combine your ride with visits to significant sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986759580/pamplona-basin-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Basin River Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic Citadel. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Cathedral and the Citadel&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/654847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Monument at Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4817230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31972862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Erro Ascent – Rabia Bridge, Zubiri loop from Pamplona/Iruña&lt;/a&gt; is nearly 99km long. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984654552/etxauri-and-guirguillano-loop-from-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxauri and Guirguillano Loop from Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 62.9 km.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes around Orcoyen?
A: The road cycling routes around Orcoyen are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from gentle slopes to demanding mountain passes, and the beautiful green hills, rivers, and forests that define the landscape.

Q: Is it possible to find circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the popular routes around Orcoyen are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984654552/etxauri-and-guirguillano-loop-from-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxauri and Guirguillano Loop from Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986649027/puerto-de-arteta-and-alto-de-ulzurrun-loop-from-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arteta and Alto de Ulzurrun Loop from Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986759580/pamplona-basin-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Basin River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, all offering convenient circular journeys.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the Etxauri and Guirguillano Loop?
A: While the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984654552/etxauri-and-guirguillano-loop-from-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxauri and Guirguillano Loop from Pamplona&lt;/a&gt; is known for its significant elevation gain and challenging nature, the route itself traverses scenic landscapes, offering views of the surrounding green hills and valleys as you navigate between the villages of Etxauri and Guirguillano.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Orcoyen area?
A: There are over 200 road cycling routes available around Orcoyen, catering to various skill levels. This includes 15 easy routes, 108 moderate routes, and 87 difficult routes, ensuring options for every type of cyclist.

Q: Can I find routes that incorporate historical sites like the Citadel of Pamplona?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986759580/pamplona-basin-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Basin River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through the Arga River area and past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4627298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Citadel of Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;. This allows you to enjoy both the natural beauty and the rich history of the region.
tips: A one-lane road with no more traffic than that of the neighbors themselves, it crosses the stone bridge over the Arga river and faces the ascent to Alto de Arraitza, a light but useful port for short training sessions.

One of the most popular road ports in Navarra. The climb from Pamplona can be a bit demanding with 10% sections, although if you do it clockwise through Belascoáin it will be a little smoother. The views from here and on the road are simply wonderful!

This section of the NA-700 highway will leave you speechless, not only because of the demanding slopes if you are coming from Pamplona, but also because the views of the valley are amazing at every turn you take.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Orkolen - Summit of Etxauri - return via the Arga River cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult road cycling route, classified as a "hard road ride." It covers approximately 62.9 kilometers with an elevation gain of 990 meters. It requires a very good level of fitness, especially for the climb to the Summit of Etxauri.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists with a good fitness level can expect to complete this route in about 3 hours and 7 minutes (11,240 seconds).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, especially on the climb to Etxauri, which generally has good road conditions and light traffic. The return via the Arga River also mostly follows paved paths. However, be aware that some segments, particularly in the broader Arga River area, might be unpaved and difficult, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. The climb to Etxauri offers impressive landscapes, and on a clear day, views of Pamplona and the green valley of the Río Arga. Specific highlights include the Stone Bridge over the Arga River, the View from the Etxauri Pass, and sections along the Etxaurri Road.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery. The Etxauri climb provides reasonably scenic views, with a beautiful steep rock face to the north and expansive valley views. The Arga River section is picturesque, following the river through Pamplona's Comarca River Park, showcasing green areas and less touristy corners of the city.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The trail description does not specify a dedicated parking area. Given its proximity to Pamplona and popularity, it's common for cyclists to start from various points in or around Pamplona. We recommend checking local parking options in Pamplona or near the start of the Etxauri climb.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for race bikes on roads and paved paths. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a challenging road cycling route like this is not typically recommended for their safety or comfort, especially given the distance and elevation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot in Navarra, and winter might bring colder temperatures or adverse weather conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the [CIMA NA08] Etxauri, The Pathway of the Arga river, Iruñerriko ibai-pasealekua, and Parque fluvial de la Comarca de Pamplona, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water stops along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through areas near Pamplona and towns, specific amenities like cafes or dedicated water stops directly on the trail are not detailed. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the climb, and plan stops in towns for refreshments.

waypoints.name: Stone Bridge over the Arga River
